name: Adverse Event Condition
description: Change to patient condition that could be an unintended effect of clinical interventions. Examples include but are not limited to rash, fracture, fever, etc.

name: Adverse Event Outcome
description: Resulting patient status or consequence following an unexpected condition change or clinical event. Examples include but are not limited to hospitalized, recovered, recovered with sequelae, and death.

name: Drug Class Allergy Intolerance
description: Pharmacologic category for an agent believed to cause a harmful or undesired physiologic response following exposure.

name: Medication Allergy Intolerance
description: Pharmacologic agent believed to cause a harmful or undesired physiologic response following exposure.

name: Non-Medication Allergy Intolerance
description: Non-pharmacologic agent believed to cause a harmful or undesired physiologic response following exposure. Examples include but are not limited to latex, eggs, pollen, and peanuts.

name: Reaction
description: Harmful or undesired physiologic response following exposure to a substance

name: Reaction Date and Time
description: Last date and time a patient had a reaction to an allergen.

name: Allergy Intolerance Onset Date and Time
description: Date and time when an allergy or intolerance developed.

name: Allergy Intolerance Recorded Date and Time
description: Date and time of documentation for the allergy.

name: Assessment and Plan of Treatment
description: Health professional's conclusions and working assumptions that will guide treatment of the patient.

name: Care Plan
description: Shared plan informed by members of a coordinated care team that details conditions, needs, and goals along with strategies for addressing them. Usage notes: Includes problems, health concerns, assessments, goals, and interventions from across care settings. Examples include but are not limited to clinical care plans, condition-specific care plans, coordinated care plan.

name: Care Team Member Identifier
description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to a member of the care team. Examples include but are not limited to National Provider Identifier (NPI) and National Council of State Boards of Nursing Identifier (NCSBN ID).

name: Care Team Member Location
description: Place where care is delivered by a care team member. Examples include but are not limited to clinic address, and location description.

name: Care Team Member Name
description: Word or phrase that constitutes the distinctive designation of a care team member. Examples include but are not limited to given name, middle name, surname, and name suffix.

name: Care Team Member Role
description: Responsibility of an individual within the care team. Examples include but are not limited to primary care physician and caregiver.

name: Care Team Member Telecom
description: Phone or email contact information for a care team member.

name: Consultation Note
description: Narrative summary of care provided in response to a request from a clinician for an opinion, advice, or service. Examples include but are not limited to dermatology, dentistry, and acupuncture.

name: Discharge Summary Note
description: Narrative summary of a patient's admission and course in a hospital or post-acute care setting. Usage note: Must contain admission and discharge dates and locations, discharge instructions, and reason(s) for hospitalization. Examples include but are not limited to dermatology discharge summary, hematology discharge summary, and neurology discharge summary.

name: Emergency Department Note
description: Narrative summary of care delivered in an emergency department.

name: History & Physical
description: Narrative summary of current and past conditions and observations used to inform an episode of care. Examples include but are not limited to admission, surgery, and other procedure.

name: Operative Note
description: Narrative summary of a surgical procedure. Usage note: May include procedures performed, operative and anesthesia times, findings observed, fluids administered, specimens obtained, and complications identified.

name: Procedure Note
description: Narrative summary of non-operative procedure. Examples include but are not limited to interventional cardiology, gastrointestinal endoscopy, and osteopathic manipulation.

name: Progress Note
description: Narrative summary of a patient's interval status during an encounter. Examples include but are not limited to hospitalization, outpatient visit, and treatment with a post-acute care provider, or other healthcare encounter.

name: Clinical Test
description: Non-imaging or non-laboratory test. Examples include but are not limited to electrocardiogram (ECG), visual acuity exam, macular exam, and graded exercise testing (GXT).

name: Clinical Test Result/Report
description: Findings of clinical tests.

name: Clinical Test Status
description: The completion status of a clinical test at the level of a single observation.

name: Communication Status
description: The current state of the communication transition.

name: Communication Topic
description: Purpose or content of communication.

name: Reason Not Communicated
description: The reason why a communication did not happen.

name: Diagnostic Imaging Reference
description: The technical details of the retrieval access path to indicate network services that can be used to access DICOM studies, series, or instances content and metadata (e.g., a DICOMweb server endpoint providing QIDO-RS, WADO-RS, or WADO-URI).

name: Diagnostic Imaging Report
description: Interpreted results of imaging tests. Usage Note: Includes both structured and unstructured (narrative) components.

name: Diagnostic Imaging Status
description: The current status of the imaging study- registered, available, cancelled, entered-in-error, or unknown.

name: Diagnostic Imaging Test
description: Tests that generate visual images and require interpretation by qualified professionals. Examples include but are not limited to computed tomography-head, radiograph-chest, and ultrasound-pelvis.

name: Diagnosis Rank
description: Ordinal number or code that identifies the priority or significance of a diagnosis for a given encounter.
notes: In QI-Core, information about principal diagnosis is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. Implementers may determine the appropriate implementation approach to support this USCDI+ Quality data element. We encourage the quality community to provide additional input on information needed to support quality measurement use cases through the HL7 ballot process for US Quality Core.

name: Encounter Diagnosis
description: Coded diagnoses associated with an episode of care.

name: Encounter Diagnosis Role
description: The specific use or role of diagnosis within the context of an encounter e.g., admitting diagnosis, principal diagnosis, discharge diagnosis, etc.

name: Encounter Disposition
description: Place or setting where the patient went after a hospital stay or encounter.

name: Encounter Identifier
description: Sequence of characters by which an encounter is known.

name: Encounter Location
description: Place where a patient's care is delivered.

name: Encounter Status
description: Current state of the encounter: Planned; Arrived; Triaged; In progress; On leave; Finished; Canceled; Entered in error; Unknown.

name: Encounter Time
description: Date/times related to an encounter. Examples include but are not limited to scheduled appointment time, check in time, and start and stop times.

name: Encounter Type
description: Category of health care service. Examples include but are not limited to office visit, telephone assessment, and home visit.

name: Present on Admission
description: An indicator of whether a patient's condition or diagnosis was known to be present at the time of admission.
notes: In QI-Core, information about present on admission is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. We have reintroduced the Present on Admission extension for Encounter.diagnosis in US Quality Core v0.5.0 and it is (USCDI+ Quality) tagged.

name: Facility Address
description: Physical location of available services or resources.

name: Facility Identifier
description: Sequence of characters representing a physical place of available services or resources.

name: Facility Name
description: Word or words by which a facility is known.

name: Facility Type
description: Category of service or resource available in a location. Examples include but are not limited to hospital, laboratory, pharmacy, ambulatory clinic, long-term and post-acute care facility, and food pantry.

name: Family Health History
description: Family member health condition(s) that are relevant to a patient's care.

name: Advance Directive Observation
description: Information about a patient or provider authored document indicating its location, content, type, and verification status. Usage note: May include structured or unstructured data, whether a person has one or more advance directive documents, the type of advance directive, the location of the document, and whether it has been verified. Such documents may be used should a person be unable to communicate their wishes, preferences, or priorities to their provider. Examples include but are not limited to an indication that a living will is on file, a reference to the location of durable medical power of attorney, and the validating provider.

name: Care Experience Preference
description: Person's goals, preferences, and priorities for overall experiences during their care and treatment. Examples include but are not limited to religious beliefs, dislikes and fears, and thoughts and feelings to be shared.

name: Patient Goals
description: Desired outcomes of patient's care. Examples include but are not limited to blood pressure control, functional ability, and nutrition.

name: SDOH Goals
description: Desired future states for an identified Social Determinants of Health-related health concern, condition, or diagnosis. Examples include but are not limited to food security, transportation security, and ability to access health care.

name: Treatment Intervention Preference
description: Person's goals, preferences, and priorities for care and treatment in case that person is unable to make medical decisions because of a serious illness or injury. Examples include but are not limited to thoughts on cardiopulmonary resuscitation, mental health treatment preferences, and thoughts on pain management.

name: Coverage Status
description: Presence or absence of health care insurance.

name: Coverage Type
description: Category of healthcare payers, insurance products, or benefits. Examples include but are not limited to Medicaid, commercial, HMO, Veterans Benefits Administration, Medicare Part D, and dental.

name: Group Identifier
description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to a specific health insurance plan.

name: Health Insurance Beneficiary
description: The party who benefits from the health insurance coverage.

name: Health Insurance Coverage Period
description: The time frame in which the policy is in force.

name: Health Insurance Payer
description: Issuer of the policy.

name: Health Insurance Plan Identifier
description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to an insurance plan.

name: Insurance Plan Name
description: Name of the health plan benefit offering assigned to the Plan Identifier.

name: Member Identifier
description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to an individual with respect to their insurance.

name: Payer Identifier
description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to an insurance payer.

name: Relationship to Subscriber
description: Relationship of a patient to the primary insured person.

name: Subscriber Identifier
description: Sequence of characters used to uniquely refer to the individual that selects insurance benefits.

name: Alcohol Use
description: Evaluation of a patient's consumption of alcohol. Examples include but are not limited to history of alcohol use, alcohol use disorder identification test and alcohol intake assessment.

name: Disability Status
description: Assessment of a patient's physical, cognitive, or psychiatric disabilities. Examples include but are not limited to American Community Survey, Veterans RAND Health Survey, and Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System (PROMIS).

name: Functional Status
description: Assessment of a person's ability to perform activities of daily living and activities across other situations and settings. Examples include but are not limited to Functional Assessment Standardized Items (FASI) and Timed Up and Go (TUG).

name: Health Concerns
description: Health-related issue or worry. Examples include but are not limited to weight gain and cancer risk.

name: Mental/Cognitive Status
description: Assessment or screening for the presence of a mental or behavioral problem. Examples include but are not limited to Confusion Assessment Method (CAM) and Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ).

name: Physical Activity
description: Evaluation of a patient's current or usual exercise. Examples include but are not limited to frequency of muscle-strengthening physical activity, days per week with moderate to strenuous physical activity, and minutes per day of moderate to strenuous physical activity.

name: Pregnancy Status
description: State or condition of being pregnant or intent to become pregnant. Examples include but are not limited to pregnant, not pregnant, and unknown.

name: SDOH Assessment
description: Screening questionnaire-based, structured evaluation for a Social Determinants of Health-related risk. Examples include but are not limited to food, housing, transportation security, and health literacy.

name: Smoking Status
description: Assessment of a patient's smoking behaviors. Examples include but are not limited to pack- years and current use.

name: Substance Use
description: Evaluation of a patient's reported use of drugs or other substances for non-medical purposes or in excess of a valid prescription. Examples include but are not limited to substance use disorder score, and substance use knowledge assessment.

name: Reason Not Performed
description: Explanation or justification provided when an order or practice guideline is not carried out. Usage note: Should be included with a procedure, immunization, and medication.

name: Immunization Record Source
description: Immunization event information source. Examples include but are not limited to facility administering the immunization and an external record.

name: Immunization Status
description: State of an immunization event.

name: Immunizations
description: Vaccine product administered, planned, or reported.

name: Lot Number
description: Sequence of characters representing a specific quantity of manufactured material within a batch of a vaccine product.

name: Vaccination Administration Date and Time
description: The date and time the vaccination event occurred.

name: Result Interpretation
description: Categorical assessment of a laboratory value, often in relation to a test's reference range. Examples include but are not limited to high, low, critical high, and normal.

name: Result Reference Range
description: Upper and lower limit of quantitative test values expected for a designated population of individuals.Usage note: Reference range values may differ by patient characteristics, laboratory test manufacturer, and laboratory test performer.

name: Result Status
description: State or condition of a laboratory test.

name: Result Unit of Measure
description: Unit of measurement to report quantitative results.

name: Specimen Condition Acceptability
description: Information about a specimen, including the container, that is used to determine a laboratory's criteria for acceptability. Usage note: This may include information about the contents of the container, the container, and the label. Examples include but are not limited to hemolyzed, clotted, container leaking, and missing patient name.

name: Specimen Identifier
description: Sequence of characters assigned by a laboratory for an individual specimen. Example includes but is not limited to accession number.

name: Specimen Source Site
description: Body location from where a specimen was obtained. Examples include but are not limited to right internal jugular, left arm, and right eye.

name: Specimen Type
description: Substance being sampled or tested. Examples include but are not limited to nasopharyngeal swab, whole blood, serum, urine, and wound swab.

name: Tests
description: Analysis of specimens derived from humans which provide information for the diagnosis, prevention,treatmentofdisease,orassessment of health.

name: Values/Results
description: Documentedfindingsofatestedspecimen including structured and unstructured components

name: Laboratory Result Report Date and Time
description: The date and time when the laboratory result(s) report is made available to inform clinical decision making.

name: Specimen Collection Date and Time
description: Date and time the test sample was collected.

name: Device Type
description: Kind of instrument, machine, appliance, implant, software, and similar medical device.

name: Unique Device Identifier (UDI)
description: Numeric or alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies a medical device. Usage note: Contains a device identifier (DI) and one or more production identifiers (PI).

name: Days Supply
description: Number of days supply of medication dispensed by the pharmacy.

name: Dispense Status
description: State of a medication with regards to dispensing or other activity. Examples include but are not limited to dispensed, partially dispensed, not dispensed.

name: Dose
description: Amount of a medication for each administration.

name: Dose Unit of Measure
description: Units of measure of a medication. Examples include but are not limited to milligram (mg) and milliliter (mL).

name: Indication
description: Sign, symptom, or medical condition that is the reason for giving or taking a medication.

name: Medication Adherence
description: Statement of whether a medication has been consumed according to instructions. Examples include but are not limited to taking as directed, taking less than directed, and not taking.

name: Medication Administration
description: Information about the event of a patient consuming or otherwise being given a medication. Examples include but are not limited to swallowing a tablet, administering an injection, and a long running infusion.

name: Medication Instructions
description: Directions for administering or taking a medication. Usage note: May include route, quantity, timing/frequency, and special instructions (PRN, sliding scale, taper). Examples include but are not limited to prescription directions for taking a medication, and package instructions for over-the-counter medications.

name: Medication Quantity
description: The amount of medication, including the unit of measure, that is prescribed or dispensed.

name: Medications
description: Pharmacologicagentusedinthediagnosis, cure, mitigation, treatment, or prevention of disease.

name: Route of Administration
description: Physiological administration path of a therapeutic agent into or onto a patient. Examples include but are not limited to oral, topical, and intravenous.

name: Date and Time Medication Administered
description: A specific date/time or interval of time during which the administration took place (or did not take place, when the 'notGiven' attribute is true).

name: Date and Time Medication Prescribed
description: The date when the prescription was initially written or authored.

name: Birth Outcome
description: The clinical status and health indicators of the newborn including clinical event during labor, delivery, and the immediate post-partum period.

name: Birth Weight
description: The weight of the infant/fetus at birth/delivery.

name: Gestational Age at Delivery
description: Gestational age at delivery refers to how far along the pregnancy is at delivery, generally expressed as a combination of weeks and days.

name: Birth Date and Time
description: The recorded date time of birth of the newborn documented in the fetal record.

name: Clinical Test Order
description: Provider-authored request for the performance of a non-laboratory or non-imaging test.

name: Diagnostic Imaging Order
description: Provider-authored request for the performance of a diagnostic imaging study.

name: Laboratory Order
description: Provider-authored request for laboratory testing.

name: Medical Device Order
description: Provider-authored request for medical devices. Examples include but are not limited to therapeutic footwear, insulin infusion pump, and continuous positive airway pressure (CPAP) machine.

name: Medication Order
description: Provider-authored request for the dispensing of a therapeutic agent.

name: Nutrition Order
description: A provider-authored request for nutrition support such as therapeutic diets, enteral feedings, parenteral nutrition, or oral nutritional supplements.

name: Portable Medical Order
description: Provider-authored request for end-of-life or life-sustaining care for a person who has a serious life-limiting medical condition. Usage note: These are meant to follow a person regardless of when and where such an order might be needed (e.g., hospital, care facility, community, home). There are variations in requirements and names for portable medical orders based on jurisdiction. Examples include, but are not limited to, POLST (Portable Medical Order for Life-Sustaining Treatment), MOLST (Medical Orders for Life-Sustaining Treatment), and out-of-hospital DNR (do-not-resuscitate).

name: Procedure Order
description: Provider-authored request for the delivery of patient care services.

name: Current Address
description: Place where a person is located or may be contacted.

name: Date of Birth
description: Known or estimated year, month, and day of the patient's birth.

name: Date of Death
description: Known or estimated year, month, and day of the patient's death.

name: Email Address
description: Unique identifier of an individual's email account that is used to send and receive email messages.

name: Ethnicity
description: Patient's self-identification as Hispanic/ Latino or Non- Hispanic/ Non-Latino.

name: First Name
description: A personal name used before a family name.

name: Interpreter Needed
description: Indication of whether a person needs language interpretation services.

name: Last Name
description: Portion of one's personal name indicating familial or communal ties.

name: Middle Name (Including middle initial)
description: A second or subsequent given name, between the first given name and the family name or surname.

name: Name Suffix
description: Name component following family name that may be used to describe a person's position in a family.

name: Occupation
description: Type of work of a person. Examples include but are not limited to infantry, business analyst, and social worker.

name: Occupation Industry
description: Type of business that compensates for work or assigns work to an unpaid worker or volunteer. Examples include but are not limited to U.S. Army, cement manufacturing, and children and youth services.

name: Patient Identifier
description: An identifier for the patient.

name: Phone Number
description: Numbers and symbols to contact an individual when using a phone.

name: Phone Number Type
description: Contact point when using a phone. Examples include but are not limited to home, work, and mobile.

name: Preferred Language
description: Documented language that the patient would like to use when discussing health care.

name: Previous Address
description: Prior place where a person may have been located or could have been contacted. Includes street name, number, city/town, state, and zip code.

name: Previous Name
description: Any first and last name an individual may have used in the past for any reason.

name: Race
description: An individual's response to the race question based upon self-identification.

name: Related Person's Name
description: Name of a person with a legal or familial relationship to a patient.

name: Relationship Type
description: Relationship of a person to a patient. Examples include but are not limited to parent, next-of-kin, guardian, and custodian.

name: Sex
description: Documentation of a specific instance of sex.

name: Tribal Affiliation
description: Tribe or band with which an individual associates.

name: Delivery Procedures
description: The method by which a baby is delivered (e.g., vaginal delivery, cesarean section).

name: Estimated Date of Delivery
description: The estimated due date (EDD) is the date that spontaneous onset of labor is expected to occur.

name: Fetal Presentation
description: The anatomical part of the fetus closest to the maternal pelvic inlet at the onset of labor.

name: Gestational Age
description: The estimated age of the pregnancy, expressed in weeks and fractions of weeks.

name: Gravidity
description: The number of pregnancies, current and past, regardless of the pregnancy outcome.

name: Parity
description: Parity is defined as the number of pregnancies reaching 20 weeks and 0 days ofgestation or beyond, including the present pregnancy

name: Pregnancy Outcomes
description: The clinical conclusion of the pregnancy focusing on the end status of the pregnancy and the clinical status of the mother.

name: Preterm Births
description: Number of births or deliveries that have occurred before 37 0/7 weeks of gestation

name: Term Births
description: Number of deliveries that have occurred at 37 0/7 weeks of gestation, or after.

name: Delivery Date and Time
description: The date and time when the newborn is completely delivered, either by full expulsion from the birth canal or full removal from the uterus, documented in the maternal record.

name: Condition Status
description: Statement of how a diagnosis, problem, or condition presents or manifests in the patient. Examples include but are not limited to active, resolved, recurrence, and remission.

name: Date of Diagnosis
description: Date of first determination by a qualified professional of the presence of a problem or condition affecting a patient.

name: Date of Onset
description: Date when a problem/condition started.

name: Date of Resolution
description: Date of subsiding or termination of a symptom, problem or condition.

name: Problems
description: Condition, diagnosis, or reason for seeking medical attention.

name: SDOH Problems/Health Concerns
description: Social Determinants of Health-related health concerns, conditions, or diagnoses. Examples include but are not limited to homelessness and food insecurity.

name: Performance Time
description: Time and/or date an activity is performed. Examples include but are not limited to vaccine or medication administration times, surgery start time, time ultrasound performed, and laboratory specimen collection time.

name: Procedure Reason Code
description: An explanation or justification for a service requested in coded or textual form.

name: Procedure Status
description: The status of planned or performed activity.

name: Procedure Type
description: The specific type of medical procedure performed.

name: Procedures
description: Activity performed for or on a patient as part of the provision of care.
notes: In QI-Core, information about principal procedure is represented using the Claim profile. In US Quality Core v1.0.0, support for the Claim profile is not required for conformance. Implementers may determine the appropriate implementation approach to support this USCDI+ Quality data element. We encourage the quality community to provide additional input on information needed to support quality measurement use cases through the HL7 ballot process for US Quality Core.

name: Reason for Referral
description: Explanation or justification for a referral or consultation.

name: SDOH Interventions
description: Actions or services to address an identified Social Determinants of Health-related health concern, condition, or diagnosis. Examples include but are not limited to education about food pantry program and referral to non- emergency medical transportation program.

name: Author
description: Actor that created or revised the data. Usage note: The actor may be a provider, a patient, a device, an outside medical record, or something else. The source of the information can be used to form assessments about its quality, reliability, trustworthiness, or can indicate where to go to determine the origins of the information.

name: Author Organization
description: Organization associated with author.

name: Author Role
description: Category of actor that participated in the creation or revision of data. Usage note: The source of the information can be used to form assessments about its quality, reliability, trustworthiness, or can indicate where to go to determine the origins of the information. Examples include but are not limited to provider, patient, family member, and device.

name: Author Time Stamp
description: Date and time of author action.

name: Referral Status
description: The current state of a referral e.g., draft, active, on-hold, revoked, completed, entered-in-error, unknown.

name: Referral Date and Time
description: Date and time when a provider wrote an order requesting services.

name: Task Code
description: Identifies the type of task that is involved.

name: Task Status
description: Thecurrent status of the task.

name: Average Blood Pressure
description: Arithmetic average of systolic and diastolic components of two of more blood pressure readings in a specified time period or according to a specified algorithm or protocol. Examples include but are not limited to 3-day morning and evening home monitoring, clinical encounter repeat average, and 24-hour ambulatory measurement.

name: BMI
description: Body mass index is a value derived from the mass and height of a person. The BMI is defined as the body mass divided by the square of the body height, and is expressed in units of kg/m², resulting from mass in kilograms and height in meters.

name: BMI Percentile (2 - 20 years)
description: BMI-for-age percentile shows how a child's weight compares to that of other children of the same age and sex.

name: Body Height
description: The vertical measurement of an individual's stature from the feet to the top of the head when standing upright.

name: Body Temperature
description: Temperature is a measure of the patient's ability to generate and get rid of heat.

name: Body Weight
description: The measurement of weight without heavy items located on the person.

name: Diastolic Blood Pressure
description: Diastolic Blood Pressure

name: Head Occipital-frontal Circumference Percentile (Birth - 36 Months)
description: Measurement of head circumference, or OFC (occipital frontal circumference), is a reflection of head growth and is a useful tool in tracking and monitoring childhood growth and development.

name: Heart Rate
description: Number of times the heart beats each minute.

name: Inhaled Oxygen Concentration
description: Inhaled oxygen concentration is the percent of oxygen inhaled (FIO2).

name: Pain Severity
description: A patient's self-reported or clinically observed assessment of the intensity of pain, expressed using a standardized scale or measure.

name: Pulse Oximetry
description: Pulse oximetry is a test used to measure the oxygen level (oxygen saturation) of the blood.

name: Respiratory Rate
description: The respiration rate is the number of breaths a person takes per minute.

name: Systolic Blood Pressure
description: Systolic Blood Pressure

name: Weight-for-length Percentile (Birth - 24 Months)
description: The higher the percentile number, the bigger a child is compared with other kids of the same age and gender, whether it's for height or weight.
